name: runtime-target
title: Choose the runtime target
description: Decide where the code will execute: which Node version, browser support range, container runtime, edge platform, or serverless target.

starter.summary: Decide where the code will execute at runtime.
starter.issue: Library choices, perf characteristics, and deployment model all depend on the runtime target. Decide early or refactor late.
assumptions: Workload profile (request/response, long-running, batch) is understood.
constraints: Cost ceilings (serverless cold starts vs always-on containers).

positions.title: Node.js 20+ on container
positions.description: Standard Node runtime in a Docker container. Predictable, broadly compatible.

positions.title: Cloudflare Workers / edge
positions.description: JavaScript/TS on V8 isolates at the edge. Near-zero cold start.

positions.title: Bun on container
positions.description: Bun runtime in a Docker container. Faster startup and bundled tooling.
pros: Built-in test runner, bundler, package manager
cons: Some Node APIs not yet supported

implications: Constrains library choices (native Node APIs vs Web APIs vs runtime-specific).
Affects deployment pipeline shape (build & push container vs upload function vs deploy worker).
Determines observability surface (container logs vs platform-managed telemetry).

notes_for_agent: If language choice is already accepted, link this DR via depends_on. Tailor positions to the language (don't offer Bun if it's a Python project). Ask about expected request volume, latency budget, and whether the team has ops headroom for managing infra.
